channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Naheed Nenshi's Apology for Meeting Israeli Ambassador Sparks Debate on Political Accountability
item.description: The article critiques Alberta Premier Rachel Notley's (later Naheed Nenshi) public apology for meeting Israeli Ambassador Iddo Moed, contrasting it with his earlier positive interactions at a Jewish c...

item.title: Scientists Identify Possible Causes of the Global Hum Phenomenon
item.description: A recent study published in PLOS ONE by researchers at the Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU) suggests that the global hum—a low-frequency sound perceived by some people worldwide—m...

item.title: Pakistani Security Forces Respond to Afghan Taliban Attacks on Kurram Border Posts
item.description: Pakistani security forces launched a full-scale retaliatory operation against the Afghan Taliban following unprovoked attacks on border posts in Kurram. The Taliban used heavy weapons, mortars, and ot...

item.title: Translator Daisy Rockwell discusses translation, Partition literature, Palestine, and the enduring influence of AK Ramanujan
item.description: In an extensive interview with Scroll, International Booker Prize-winning translator Daisy Rockwell reflects on her work as a translator, writer and artist, explaining how themes such as Partition, co...

item.title: Hawthorn Hawks dominate Richmond in AFL clash
item.description: Hawthorn delivered a crushing 70-point victory over Richmond at the MCG, propelling them back into the top four of the AFL ladder. Uncontracted forward Mitch Lewis starred with five goals, while Nick ...

item.title: Hockey Remains Canada's Favorite Sport Despite FIFA World Cup Exposure, Survey Shows
item.description: A recent Leger survey conducted for the Association of Canadian Studies reveals that hockey remains Canada's most popular sport despite the increased global attention on the FIFA World Cup. The survey...

item.title: Speaker Of House Denies Rift With Kaduna Governor, Vows Continued Collaboration
item.description: Speaker of the House, Abbas Tajudeen, has officially denied rumors of a rift with Kaduna State Governor Uba Sani, assuring supporters and residents of Zaria that their partnership remains strong. The ...

item.title: Potential Tropical Cyclone May Intensify Habagat Ahead of Weekend
item.description: A potential tropical cyclone is expected to enhance the Habagat, a seasonal weather phenomenon in the Philippines, as it approaches towards the weekend. The development of this cyclone could lead to i...

item.title: Residents protest development plans for historic Parnham House in Beaminster
item.description: Residents of Beaminster are opposing plans to develop the historic Parnham House, dubbed the 'Buckingham Palace of Dorset', which is a Grade I-listed Elizabethan mansion. The proposed development incl...

item.title: Celebrity Chef Parveen Ashraf Addresses Tragic Loss of Husband Killed in Cold Blood in Ghana
item.description: Parveen Ashraf, a renowned celebrity chef, has made her first public statement following the tragic and shocking murder of her husband, who was shot in cold blood in front of their son in Ghana. The i...

item.title: Two Arrested for Molotov Attack on Tasmac in Krishnagiri
item.description: Two individuals were arrested for hurling a Molotov cocktail at a Tasmac outlet in Krishnagiri district on Sunday evening. The suspects, identified as Sathiyamoorthy (27) and Thirumal alias Nitish Kum...

item.title: Delhi Police Denies Permission for CJP Protest March to Parliament on July 20
item.description: The Delhi Police has officially stated that no permission was sought or granted for the planned protest march by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) towards Parliament on July 20. The protest is part of a...

item.title: Multan Police Book Javed Hashmi and Three Sons-In-Law in Power Theft Cases
item.description: Multan police have booked veteran politician Javed Hashmi and his three sons-in-law in four cases related to alleged electricity theft. The FIRs, registered under Section 462J of the Pakistan Penal Co...

item.title: Efforts to Preserve Somalia’s Radio Mogadishu Archive Amid Deterioration
item.description: A team in Somalia is working to digitize and preserve over 400,000 hours of broadcasts from Radio Mogadishu, the country's oldest public radio station. Founded in 1951 during colonial rule, the archiv...

item.title: Weather access issues and technical troubleshooting steps
item.description: The article discusses a technical issue preventing users from accessing weather information for Traralgon and Morwell. News Corp Australia explains that their websites use software to manage crawler b...

item.title: Prince Adeyemi Transferred to Cybercrime Centre Amid Legal Battle with Tinubu's Chief of Staff
item.description: Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi Matthew, who previously accused President Bola Tinubu's Chief of Staff, Femi Gbajabiamila, of bribery, has been moved from the Nigeria Police Force's Intelligence Response Team ...

item.title: World Cup Players Who Struggled: A Review of Key Performances
item.description: As the FIFA World Cup concludes, several high-profile players failed to meet expectations, contributing to their teams' early exits. Brazilian defender Gabriel Magalhães, despite featuring in all five...

item.title: Tanzania urged to diversify energy sources amid global fuel price volatility
item.description: Economic experts in Tanzania are urging the government to accelerate natural gas infrastructure development and build strategic fuel reserves to mitigate the impact of geopolitical tensions on fuel pr...

item.title: Protesters Plan to Humiliate Trump at World Cup Trophy Ceremony
item.description: Protesters are organizing to humiliate U.S. President Donald Trump during the World Cup trophy presentation at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey. A coalition of artists and activists has distributed hundr...

item.title: Canberra property sales remain strong despite quiet auction weekend
item.description: Despite a quieter auction market over the weekend, Canberra property sales continue to show resilience, with many vendors securing strong results by selling properties before scheduled auctions. The t...
